title: bus.agent.run.eval v1
description:
A SYNTHESIS EVALUATION — the verdict the Reflexarc synthesis pass (b7 / mgv7z) reaches about ONE recurring behavioral issue, plus the result of replaying a candidate deterministic fix against history. Emitted once per (issue_signature) per synthesis pass. Member of the bus.agent.* family — flat data-only; the CloudEvents-lite envelope is applied by `nervous publish`.

WHY THIS EXISTS: the Tier-1 detectors raise candidates into the issues/detector_hits tables (Kyoko prevalence + recurrence). Synthesis ranks those issues, climbs each to the highest Remediation-Ladder rung (Eliminate > Automate > Inform), and — critically — GATES a proposed fix behind a replay-eval: a counterfactual re-run proving the fix would have prevented the historical firings WITHOUT falsely suppressing legitimate behavior. run.eval is the durable record of that decision; a `decision==propose_fix` eval is what licenses emitting <project>.pattern.discovered.v1 (and optionally filing a proposal bead).

AUDITABILITY IS ENFORCED, NOT CONVENTIONAL (2026-06-13 adversarial audit): the conditionals at the bottom of this schema make a verdict reconstructable from required fields. When the replay gate ran (status passed/failed) the count trio (runs_evaluated, would_have_prevented, false_suppression) + prevention_rate are REQUIRED, so a 'passed'/'suppressed' verdict cannot be emitted without its evidence; a 'passed' gate must have runs_evaluated>=1 (cannot pass against nothing). decision_rationale, labeled_support (+ its three buckets), and scorer_version are REQUIRED so the call, the (thin) label support, and score-comparability are always present.

GROUNDED IN THE LIVE STREAM (2026-06-13): outcome labels are SPARSE (~10% of runs labeled; the rest close on idle_timeout unlabeled). Therefore synthesis MUST rank primarily on deterministic detector signal (prevalence x recurrence x ladder-rung), using outcome labels only as a refinement/confirmation boost — never as a gate. `labeled_support` carries the (small) labeled breakdown explicitly, with `unlabeled` its own required bucket, so a consumer can see how thin the label confirmation is and so null can never be silently folded into clean. Runs that fragmented across idle_timeout are expected to be re-stitched (continues_run_id) BEFORE counting, so a single logical run is not double-counted in prevalence/recurrence.

eval_id.description: ULID for this evaluation record (sortable, monotonic, 26 chars). Primary key; a new eval is produced each synthesis pass that re-evaluates the signature.

project.description: Project the issue belongs to (repo dir name, or 'main'). The keying axis for cross-run learning, mirrored from the issue.

issue_signature.description: The stable issue signature being evaluated — '<project>:<detector>:<stable_anchor>', the issues-table primary key. Contains NO run_id and NO timestamp (that is what lets recurrence dedup work). This is the identity a fix is proposed against and that replay is gated on.

pattern_name.description: Human-readable pattern name (e.g. 'worktree_leak', 'rebuild_cache_miss'), mirrored from the detector candidate. REQUIRED when decision==propose_fix (the licensed emission must be fully specified by the eval).

detector.description: Detector that raised the issue (DETECTOR_NAME). When multiple detectors corroborate one signature, the primary detector; see `corroborating_detectors`.

corroborating_detectors.description: Other detectors whose hits reinforce this issue. Empty/absent when only one detector fired.

synthesis_pass_at.description: RFC3339 UTC — when this synthesis pass produced the eval.

score.description: Composite ranking score (higher = more worth fixing). A monotonic function of score_components; the absolute scale is internal but comparable ONLY within the same `scorer_version`. Used to order proposals.

score_components.description: TRANSPARENT breakdown of `score` so a human/auditor can see WHY an issue ranked. Synthesis must populate every component it used; a consumer can re-derive `score` from these.

prevalence.description: Kyoko #2: distinct hit LOGICAL-runs / total logical runs for this (project, detector) over the scoring window (0..1).

recurrence_count.description: Kyoko #5: issues.recurrence_count — distinct-firing recurrences (NOT synthesis-pass count).

ladder_rung_weight.description: Weight from the achievable remediation rung — Eliminate > Automate > Inform. Higher rungs score higher because they are both more valuable AND more measurable (replay-testable), which self-reinforces climbing the ladder.

label_confirmation.description: Additive refinement term from sparse outcome labels (positive toward failure outcomes, ~0 unlabeled, slightly negative toward confirmed clean). ADDITIVE ONLY — never a multiplier that can zero or dominate the score, since labels are too sparse to gate on.

recency.description: Decay factor favoring issues seen recently (issues.last_seen) over stale ones.

window_days.description: Rolling window (days) over which prevalence/recency/replay were computed.

labeled_support.description: EXPLICIT label breakdown of the LOGICAL runs touching this issue, gating on labeled_at (per the run.closed null-vs-clean rule). REQUIRED, with all three buckets REQUIRED, so `unlabeled` can never be silently omitted or folded into clean. `unlabeled` is expected to dominate — it carries no outcome signal.

confirmed_failures.description: Runs labeled thrashed/abandoned/reverted (labeled_at NOT NULL) exhibiting this issue.

confirmed_clean.description: Runs labeled clean/landed/corrected (labeled_at NOT NULL) exhibiting this issue. A high count is a SUPPRESSION signal — the behavior may be benign.

unlabeled.description: Runs exhibiting the issue with labeled_at IS NULL. Usually the majority; carries no outcome signal.

rung.description: The HIGHEST achievable Remediation-Ladder rung for this issue's fix, after synthesis climbed it. Synthesis may downgrade a detector's rung (e.g. on replay failure) but must NEVER fabricate a higher one.

rung_descent_reason.description: If `rung` is below 'eliminate', the explicit non-null reason the higher rung was not achievable (the ladder discipline: record WHY you fell). MUST be null when rung=='eliminate' and a non-null string when rung is 'automate' or 'inform' (enforced by conditional below).

remediation.description: The concrete proposed fix at `rung`. Mirrors/sharpens the detector's proposed_remediation. REQUIRED when decision==propose_fix.

proposal.description: Human-readable description of the deterministic fix.

target.description: Machine-actionable target of the fix — shares the {type, target_selector, content} shape with <project>.pattern.discovered.v1 proposed_patch so the two never drift.

type.description: Kind of change. 'env'/'config' typically Eliminate (e.g. CARGO_TARGET_DIR); 'skill'/'rule' typically Automate; 'doc' typically Inform.

target_selector.description: Where the change lands (file path, settings key, hook id, etc.). Same field name as pattern.discovered.proposed_patch.target_selector.

replay_gate.description: THE AUTOMATE REPLAY-EVAL GATE — the measurable counterfactual that licenses (or blocks) a fix. Synthesis replays the candidate fix against the historical (re-stitched, signature-scoped) firings and measures whether it would have prevented the issue WITHOUT suppressing legitimate behavior. The conditionals below make the gate self-justifying: a passed/failed status MUST carry the count trio + prevention_rate, and a passed gate MUST have evaluated >=1 run.

status.description: passed = simulated fix prevents the historical firings with acceptable false-suppression; failed = it does not, OR it suppresses legitimate runs (DO NOT propose); not_applicable = rung==inform (nothing deterministic to replay) — note 'measurable in principle but replay not yet implemented' is a DISTINCT case and must NOT use not_applicable; insufficient_history = too few historical runs to evaluate; pending = gate not yet run.

method.description: How the counterfactual was evaluated, scoped to THIS signature (e.g. 're-evaluated the rebuild_cache_miss firings for crate X over the window with a shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR simulated; a firing is preventable iff an earlier in-window build of X exists').

runs_evaluated.description: Number of historical (re-stitched, signature-scoped) firing runs the replay evaluated. Must be >=1 when status==passed.

would_have_prevented.description: Counterfactual: how many of those firings the simulated fix would have eliminated.

false_suppression.description: Firings where the simulated fix would have WRONGLY suppressed/altered legitimate behavior (e.g. removing a worktree whose branch is not merged). Distinct from 'not preventable' (e.g. a first-ever build a shared cache simply can't help — that is neither prevented nor a false suppression). A non-trivial value FAILS the gate.

not_preventable.description: Firings the fix legitimately cannot help but does NOT harm (e.g. first-ever crate build under a shared-cache fix). Excluded from the false_suppression that fails the gate, and excluded from the prevention_rate denominator so the most-important Eliminate fixes are not structurally blocked by inherently-unpreventable cases.

prevention_rate.description: would_have_prevented / (would_have_prevented + harmful firings) over the evaluated, preventable-eligible set. The headline measurable. REQUIRED when status is passed/failed.

decision.description: The synthesis verdict. propose_fix = gate passed (or inform above its higher bar) -> emit <project>.pattern.discovered.v1. monitor = real but below the act bar. suppressed = gate failed or confirmed_clean dominates -> explicitly DO NOT propose. needs_more_data = insufficient_history / too few runs.

decision_rationale.description: REQUIRED. Short justification tying score + replay_gate + labeled_support to the decision, so the call is auditable without re-deriving it.

supersedes_eval_id.description: The prior eval_id for this issue_signature, if this pass re-evaluated it; null on first evaluation. Pair with scorer_version + synthesis_pass_at to tell 'verdict moved because evidence grew' from 'because the scorer changed'.

supersede_reason.description: Optional: why this eval supersedes its predecessor, so a consumer need not diff two full records.

run_sample.description: A bounded sample of LOGICAL run ids exhibiting the issue, for evidence/drill-down. Not exhaustive.

scorer_version.description: REQUIRED monotonic version of the scoring+gate function that produced this eval. `score` values are comparable ONLY within the same scorer_version; a required version is the only thing that keeps proposal ordering well-defined across passes and lets old evals be re-scored deterministically.

schema_version.description: Major schema version (semver major only, per repo convention).

allOf.description: Gate evidence is mandatory whenever the gate actually ran: a passed/failed verdict cannot be emitted without its counts + rate.
A passed gate must have evaluated at least one run (cannot 'pass' against nothing).
insufficient_history must still report how many runs were available.
Eliminate/Automate rungs have a deterministic fix -> the gate MUST have engaged (not not_applicable). not_applicable is reserved for inform's intrinsic unmeasurability.
Ladder-descent audit: a non-eliminate rung must record WHY it fell; eliminate must not carry a descent reason.
A propose_fix verdict must be a complete, self-contained justification of the licensed emission.
